title: Sour Dense Show-off Kenyan Padron Peppers Stir-fry
description: A spicy and tangy whole30-friendly dish featuring padron peppers, which are often considered a 'fiery' snack but here served in a more subdued, yet flavorful way.

ingredients.name: Padron Peppers
ingredients.notes: Choose peppers that are firm and have a slight heat. Remove the seeds if you prefer milder spiciness.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Sliced thinly for a nice texture contrast with the peppers.

ingredients.name: Thai Chili Sauce or Sambal Oelek
Apple Cider Vinegar
ingredients.notes: Used for its acidity to balance out the spiciness and sweetness of the dish.

ingredients.name: Coconut Sugar or Honey (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a slight sweetness that complements the tartness of vinegar and chili sauce.

ingredients.name: Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: To add brightness and acidity to the dish.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing vegetables and adding moisture to the stir-fry.

instructions: Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onions and garlic, stirring until translucent.
Toss in padron peppers. Cook for about 5 minutes or until they start to brown slightly.
Pour Thai chili sauce or sambal oelek into the pan with peppers and onions, mix well.
Gradually add apple cider vinegar while constantly stirring. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes.
If desired, add coconut sugar or honey (or maple syrup) to balance out the acidity of the vinegar and chili sauce.
Garnish with a splash of fresh lemon juice just before serving.
Serve hot as a side dish with your choice of protein.
